response.justification: Wei Chen brings genuine distributed-systems depth — the Dunnhumby role handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O is directly relevant — and current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) maps well to the AI-native environment expectation. However, Python is a stated must-have technical skill and is entirely absent from the resume and skills list, which is a meaningful gap at staff level. Stakeholder management and explicit incident-response ownership are also underrepresented. The candidate warrants a phone screen to assess Python exposure, but should not advance without it being resolved.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience: low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Active AI-agent infrastructure contributions at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling — direct fit with the AI-native engineering baseline the role requires
15+ years of polyglot, full-stack ownership across web, platform, and embedded domains, with consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development practices
concerns: Python is a must-have technical requirement and does not appear anywhere in the candidate's skills list or employment history — no evidence of even working-level proficiency

Cross-functional stakeholder management and technical strategy at scale are not demonstrated; the profile skews toward individual technical contribution rather than driving decisions across product and leadership
No explicit mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational ownership, all of which are stated requirements for this role
